synthesis_text: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS‑CoV‑2 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000][doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00024]; the World Health Organization declared it a global health emergency in March 2020 and ended the emergency in May 2023 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000].

COVID-19 is an illness caused by the SARS‑CoV‑2 virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]. The virus began spreading at the end of 2019 and was declared a pandemic in 2020 [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]. It spreads mainly through close contact via respiratory droplets when an infected person coughs, sneezes, talks, or breathes [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007]. Most infected people experience mild or no symptoms, but older adults and individuals with certain medical conditions are at higher risk of developing severe illness [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_000].
COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by the SARS‑CoV‑2 coronavirus; the term stands for corona, virus, disease [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00000].
